chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Mutual Inheritance of a Man and a Woman Whom He Marries and Then Divorces During His Illness

text: 5668 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْعَبَّاسِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا طَلَّقَ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلْمَرْأَةَ فِي مَرَضِهِ وَرِثَتْهُ مَا دَامَ فِي مَرَضِهِ ذَلِكَ وَ إِنِ اِنْقَضَتْ عِدَّتُهَا إِلاَّ أَنْ يَصِحَّ مِنْهُ " قُلْتُ فَإِنْ طَالَ بِهِ اَلْمَرَضُ قَالَ "تَرِثُهُ مَا بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ سَنَةٍ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5668 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Jamil ibn Darraj, from Abu Al-Abbas, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "If a man divorces his wife while he is ill, she inherits from him as long as he remains in that illness, even if her waiting period (iddah) has ended - unless he recovers from his illness."
I asked: "What if his illness lasts for a long time?"
Imam (as) replied: "She inherits from him for up to one year."
